signal_zone_description.zone_1_description: Front entrance glass door contact – primary customer entrance facing Commercial Drive; triggers on forced entry or if door opened while system is armed.
signal_zone_description.zone_2_description: Rear staff entrance steel door contact – opens to alley used for deliveries and staff access outside customer hours.
signal_zone_description.zone_3_description: Main sales floor motion detector – wide-angle PIR covering pastry display cases, cash counter, and seating area.
signal_zone_description.zone_4_description: Cash counter panic/hold-up button – hardwired, latching; located under main POS counter within easy reach of staff.
signal_zone_description.zone_5_description: Back hallway motion detector – monitors corridor between kitchen, walk-in cooler, and rear exit door.
signal_zone_description.zone_6_description: Walk-in cooler door contact – monitors unauthorized access to refrigerated storage area outside normal operating hours.
signal_zone_description.zone_7_description: Kitchen side window contact – street-facing window near dishwashing station; vulnerable to break-and-enter attempts.
signal_zone_description.zone_8_description: Office door contact – private office containing safe, petty cash, and business records located at rear of premises.
signal_zone_description.zone_9_description: Office motion detector – interior motion inside office to provide backup detection if door is forced or left ajar.
signal_zone_description.zone_10_description: Smoke/heat detector over baking area – monitored fire/smoke sensor located near ovens and proofing cabinets.
signal_zone_description.zone_11_description: Smoke detector in storage room – monitored smoke sensor covering dry goods and packaging storage area.
signal_zone_description.zone_12_description: Glass-break detector covering front display windows – detects impact/frequency of breaking glass along storefront.
signal_zone_description.zone_13_description: Overhead bay door contact – monitors sectional overhead door used for bulk flour and ingredient deliveries.
signal_zone_description.zone_14_description: Low-temperature sensor in walk-in cooler – sends alert if temperature rises above preset threshold indicating possible equipment failure.
signal_zone_description.zone_15_description: System tamper – panel and keypad tamper switches to detect opening or removal of alarm equipment enclosures.
signal_zone_description.zone_16_description: AC power failure – sends signal if main AC power to alarm panel is lost for extended period (after programmed delay).

special_instructions.special_instructions_line_1: Normal business hours are Monday to Friday 06:00–19:00 and Saturday/Sunday 07:00–18:00. Staff typically arrive 30–45 minutes before opening to start baking (ovens, mixers) and may remain up to 60 minutes after closing to clean and complete cash-out. Opening and closing signals outside these windows may be due to early-morning wholesale orders or special catering pickups, but if an opening occurs between 22:30 and 04:30, please treat as unusual and contact the primary keyholder (Evelyn Chen) for verification before disregarding any alarm activity.
special_instructions.special_instructions_line_2: If an intrusion, motion, or glass-break alarm occurs during closed hours and you cannot reach Evelyn first, please proceed in this order: (1) Call Daniel Morales (assistant manager) who lives 10 minutes away and usually responds quickly; (2) If Daniel does not answer, call Priya Singh; (3) If no keyholder can be reached and there are multiple alarms (e.g., front door + interior motion or glass-break + motion), please dispatch police as per account settings and leave detailed voicemail messages for Evelyn and Daniel describing which zones were triggered and at what times. Note that the bakery has visible mixers and espresso equipment that have been the target of theft in nearby businesses.
special_instructions.special_instructions_line_3: For fire/smoke or low-temperature alarms, please treat as high priority. Fire/smoke: immediately notify fire department and then call Evelyn, followed by Daniel. Low-temperature in walk-in cooler: call Evelyn regardless of time of day, as loss of refrigeration for more than a few hours can ruin high-value perishable inventory (butter, cream, eggs). Premises are dog-free and there are no overnight occupants; any interior motion after closing should be considered suspicious. If you receive repeated brief power-failure signals during severe storms, please attempt to contact Evelyn to confirm whether BC Hydro has reported known outages in the area before dispatching a guard purely for power loss. The alarm keypad is located just inside the front entrance to the right; most staff may appear on camera turning the system off within 30 seconds of entry during normal openings.
